programmes.show_description: Sir Henry asks Joyce for a favour. Tiger and Joey are up for the hero award. Liam is back to visit Les and is interested in a job at the salon. S8 E7
Terry gets another dead-end job when Arthur agrees to collect a coffin from the airport, but it's clear that something fishy is going on. S3 E1: Dead Men Do Tell Tales
Alf acts strangely after being locked out of his bedroom by Mrs Hollingberry. The milkman arrives with good news. S2 E3
Robin and Chrissy have borrowed the Ropers' car to drive to Southampton for an Arsenal match, but the car breaks down and they miss the game. S4 E1: Home & Away
When David buys a raffle ticket in aid of Conservative funds, Labour supporter Amy is shocked and accuses him of selling out the socialist principles he embraced when they met. S3 E6: Cause Celebre
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Eli is planning a weekend away while Nellie wants to decorate the cellar, but an error means that Eli and Nellie are stuck together for the weekend. S3 E5: Getting To Know You
With growing competition, the factory faces closure, leaving Eli and Nellie penniless, starving, and with no staff. S3 E6: Two Pennies To Rub Together
We make the spectacular journey from Lancaster to Barrow-in-Furness on the historic and beautiful Furness Railway exploring its history, the area's mining heritage, and the sights along the way.

Arthur buys a greyhound to get rich with and gets conned over some dodgy video recorders - however, the police might be too busy fighting with each other to notice. S5 E9: Around the Corner
With Elizabeth in New York, Lady Marjorie prepares to visit - meanwhile, James takes an interest in Richard's new secretary. S3 E1: Miss Forrest
Adrian receives a letter from an 18-year-old fan of his poems and arranges to meet her. Meanwhile, Mrs Boswell meets up with Derek again. S5 E11
Harold is intending to go on a skiing holiday to Austria on his own, but Albert feels lonely and neglected while Harold gets some last-minute practice in. S5 E2: A Winter's Tale
A horsebox belonging to Lady Lawrence is delivered to the station - and Wilfred is on hand with the essentials - while Harry's signal box seems to be on fire. S1 E6: Horse Play
Mr Spooner annoys the household by playing his radio loudly - Rigsby is annoyed, but he finds it difficult to pluck up the courage to confront him about it. S1 E5: All Our Yesterdays
The morning after a party, Chrissy and Jo find Robin asleep in their bath and ask him to become their third roommate. S1 E1: Three's a Crowd
With a child of their own on the way, Yvonne thinks Gary would do well to get some practice in by minding their 6 year old nephew, Arthur. S4 E2: In The Mood
When Jack agrees to help Manners buy a house from Lord Calderbeck by acting as a frontman, Billy - who is working on a nearby farm - helps Jack obtain inside information. S1 E10: King for a Day
Arthur buys a greyhound to get rich with and gets conned over some dodgy video recorders - however, the police might be too busy fighting with each other to notice. S5 E9: Around the Corner
John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny reluctantly accepts an invitation to dinner from her estranged husband Graham. S2 E3: Meeting Penny's Husband
Robin and Chrissy have borrowed the Ropers' car to drive to Southampton for an Arsenal match, but the car breaks down and they miss the game. S4 E1: Home & Away
Classic sitcom about one-man disaster area Frank Spencer. Frank finds it hard to believe that the council has condemned their old house. S3 E1: Moving House
Sharon's disapproval of Tracey dating policeman Barry and apparently overlooking Garth's feelings for his dad leads to a major quarrel between the sisters. S12 E3: Tombstone Blues
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Harold is intending to go on a skiing holiday to Austria on his own, but Albert feels lonely and neglected while Harold gets some last-minute practice in. S5 E2: A Winter's Tale
Rigsby is given some advice from Alan and Philip - as well as some tablets - to calm his nerves in his amorous pursuit of Miss Jones. S1 E4: Charisma

Bob's married life promises work, worry and wallpapering; Terry wastes the days away in the pub and the bookies as happy as ever. Bob wonders who has the right answer. S2 E3: The Ant & The Grasshopper
To make room in the lock-up, Arthur and Ray swap its contents for a fruit machine filled with stolen money.
The Dawsons are back for another all-inclusive holiday and Eddie makes a shattering entrance. Kenneth is shocked by the appearance of an old friend of Uncle Herbert, played by Nigel Havers. S9 E1
Jamie hospitalises Joe when he finds out he's been hitting Mandy. Maxine has a plan to help Debbie manage the family on nothing, and Micky finds himself on the wrong end of a pronged baton. S6 E11
When suspected woodworm is discovered in the upstairs flat, the girls and Robin have to find alternative accommodation while George calls in his so-called handyman. S4 E3: All In The Game
New resident Ambrose claims he has the power of hypnosis due to his gypsy blood - Rigsby is sceptical but soon wonders if Ambrose's abilities could be used in his favour. S4 E5: Under The Influence
The Dawsons are back for another all-inclusive holiday and Eddie makes a shattering entrance. Kenneth is shocked by the appearance of an old friend of Uncle Herbert, played by Nigel Havers. S9 E1
Arthur takes on some hired help when Terry is injured - and unwittingly finds himself mixed up in drug dealing. S3 E2: You Need Hands
Alf and Arthur enjoy a pint discussing the free society. Alf reckons you can have a free society - but only with control and discipline. S2 E4
Chrissy is practising for her driving test and is receiving lessons from Robin; meanwhile, George has started making home brew liquor which turns out stronger than expected. S4 E2: One for the Road
When the hotel throws a party where the guests must adopt fancy dress and perform on stage, Amy dresses as a clown and turns David and Linda's romantic duet into a total farce. S3 E7: Party Night
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
It is Boxing Day in the Pledge house, Bored Lily and Walter join Nellie and Eli in a seance. S3 E7: The Ghost of Picklers Past
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E1: A Price on Your Head
The Welsh Highland Railway is the UK's longest heritage railway and runs for 25 miles through the mountains of North Wales between the coastal ports of Porthmadog and Caernarvon.

A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Five years have passed when Bob and Terry meet on the last train home by chance and the events of the past few years flood out. S1 E1: Stranger on a Train
Thelma isn't happy that Terry and Bob have rekindled their friendship and is worried that Terry will cause Bob to have second thoughts about their wedding. S1 E2: Home Is the Hero
The pilot episode of the 'upstairs downstairs' sitcom by the writers of Hi-de-Hi! Alf and his daughter Ivy enter service at the Meldrum house, but Alf gets a shock after seeing a familiar face.
Classic sitcom about one-man disaster area Frank Spencer. Frank finds it hard to believe that the council has condemned their old house. S3 E1: Moving House
Classic sitcom featuring the accident-prone Frank Spencer and his long-suffering wife. Frank's attempt at building Jessica a doll's house in woodwork class gets out of hand. S3 E2: Wendy House
Classic sitcom. When Betty comes home after a dance lesson with their neighbour, Frank becomes suspicious of his intentions and insists on getting involved. S3 E3: Scottish Dancing
Rigsby is looking for another lodger - one of his current lodgers, Miss Jones, is keen for her acquaintance Philip to move in because she is attracted to him. S1 E1: The New Lodger
Rigsby becomes convinced that magic is real after Philip bangs a ritual spear on the floor - while Ruth tries to offer herself to Rigsby, Philip and Alan play a trick on him. S1 E2: Black Magic
Rigsby is dressed to kill as he takes Miss Jones out to dinner to celebrate her birthday. S1 E3: A Night Out
Rigsby is given some advice from Alan and Philip - as well as some tablets - to calm his nerves in his amorous pursuit of Miss Jones. S1 E4: Charisma
Mr Spooner annoys the household by playing his radio loudly - Rigsby is annoyed, but he finds it difficult to pluck up the courage to confront him about it. S1 E5: All Our Yesterdays
When Miss Jones sees a prowler through her window, Rigsby suspects everyone else as a police detective appears and begins to investigate. S1 E6: The Prowler
An election polarises the household - Rigsby supports the Conservatives, Alan and Philip lean towards Labour, and Miss Jones is impressed with the Liberal candidate. S1 E7: Stand Up and Be Counted
Harold makes plans to sail around the world with a group of young adventurers, but he has to solve the problem of what to do with Albert. S3 E1: Homes Fit For Heroes

Harold buys a job lot of coffins and stores them in the living room; Albert strongly objects and moves into the stable to sleep, leaving Harold alone in the house at night. S3 E2: The Wooden Overcoats
Cash flow problems cause Harold to buy some lead at a knockdown price from a stranger who visits the yard, but panic sets in when the police pay a visit. S3 E3: The Lead Man Cometh
Harold meets a French au pair on his rounds and starts dating her, but a revelation from Albert's past means Harold's relationship with Monique may be too close for comfort. S3 E4: Steptoe a La Cart
The Steptoes go for their weekly visit to the cinema; unfortunately, their tastes in big screen entertainment differ wildly. S3 E5: Sunday For Seven Days
Arthur and Terry help newsagent Mr Mukerjee, who is receiving death threats due to a vendetta involving his daughter and her being promised for arranged marriages. S1 E7: The Bengal Tiger
Arthur falls out with Dave when a member of their boyhood gang reappears on the scene. S9 E6: Gone With the Winchester
Joyce has been invited to a conference in Barcelona. It seems like a welcome break - until she finds out that she has to make a speech in Spanish. Can Mateo help her pronunciation? S9 E2
Ian discovers that Danny is responsible for Liam skipping school and suspects that there is more to it than meets the eye. Meanwhile, Patrick goes cold turkey. S6 E12
Larry gives girls fake names; the most recent one he uses is Robin Tripp, so Chrissy and Jo think Robin is a real bounder when a girl turns up saying she is pregnant. S4 E4: Never Give Your Real Name
Rigsby is looking for another lodger - one of his current lodgers, Miss Jones, is keen for her acquaintance Philip to move in because she is attracted to him. S1 E1: The New Lodger
